title: This is what we do | 2026 National Veterans Wheelchair Games
summary:
This year, the National Veterans Wheelchair Games comes to Detroit, Michigan. Celebrating it's 45th year, the The Wheelchair Games, co-presented by U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs and Paralyzed Veterans of America, is the world’s largest annual wheelchair rehabilitation sports event solely for military Veterans living with disabilities.

In this video, the week has just begun and we check in with Veterans arriving at the airport and getting oriented with registration at the event. This is what we do, but we're just getting started. 

VA Adaptive Sports & Arts Programs:
https://department.va.gov/veteran-sports/

Paralyzed Veterans of America (PVA):
http://www.pva.org/

Wheelchair Games:
https://wheelchairgames.org/

@Sports4Vets on Facebook, Instagram, X
@ParalyzedVeterans1946

title: Lethality in the Pacific: P-735 Machine Gun Range hits major target fielding milestone at Marine Corps Base Camp Blaz
summary: Warfighters in the United States Pacific Command theater are now able to use a premier live-fire training facility to enhance their combat readiness. On June 18, 2026, Program Manager Training Systems, supported by Naval Surface Warfare Center Corona, completed the fielding of target systems for the P-735 Multi-Purpose Machine Gun Range at Marine Corps Base Camp Blaz. The TRASYS and RS10L2F team reached a critical milestone with the successful completion of both the Government Acceptance Test and New Equipment Training for the range, which is located at the Mason Range Complex.

title: 40 Years of Aging Strong | 2026 National Veterans Golden Age Games
summary:
That is a wrap on the 2026 National Veterans Golden Age Games. Inspired by programs that promote healthy aging, fitness and recreation, VA initiated the National Veterans Golden Age Games in 1985 to underscore the preventive and therapeutic value of sports medicine for older Veterans who receive health care at VA facilities. Aging strong for 40 years, the games have encouraged physical activity through competitive sports for positive outcomes and have been promoting better health and wellness. 

Enjoy highlights from this year's event held in Tampa, Florida. 

More info at: 
https://department.va.gov/veteran-sports/

title: Supreme Court Ruling JUST Changed Second Amendment Rights
summary:
The U.S. Supreme Court just delivered a major Second Amendment ruling that clears up critical gray areas for law enforcement officers carrying off-duty.

In this episode, Chief Mark Garrett breaks down the Wolford v. Lopez decision out of Hawaii, how it builds directly on Bruen, and why it removes the default prohibition that put active and retired LEOs in legal jeopardy in restrictive states. From LEOSA protections to real liability implications for departments, this one matters.

title: Marines, Sailors of Littoral Combat Force support U.S. interagency earthquake response in Venezuela
summary: Following two earthquakes off the northern coast of Venezuela on June 24 the United States mobilized a humanitarian response at the request of the Venezuelan government. To support U.S. Southern Command’s rapid deployment of personnel and specialized equipment into the disaster zone, Littoral Combat Force 24 transitioned from scheduled regional security missions to support the U.S. government’s disaster response operation.

title: The 2011 Realignment of Adult Felony Populations: Results and Key Takeaways
summary: In 2011, California shifted responsibility for incarcerating and supervising certain adult felony populations from the state to counties. The state also shifted a portion of tax revenues to counties to cover the additional costs. These changes were part of a larger shift of responsibilities known as the 2011 realignment. Pursuant to Chapter 96 of 2023 (AB 1080, Ta), this report provides information on how various aspects of the realignment of adult felony populations have been implemented and evaluates the results.

title: SCOTUS 6-3: TPS is TEMPORARY – Trump Admin Can Now Act
summary:
The Supreme Court just ruled that “temporary” actually means temporary, and Democrats are losing their minds over it.

In this episode we break down the 6-3 decision that gives DHS full authority to end TPS designations, why 13 of the 17 countries are losing protections, and how the left’s multiculturalism agenda just took a major hit. We also expose the hotel industry scare tactics and the same old “dangerous countries” talking points from border-state politicians who still can’t define basic words like “border.”

title: 3rd Marine Littoral Regiment, 4th Marine Brigade conduct bilateral littoral mobility training during KAMANDAG 10
summary: U.S. Marines with 3rd Marine Littoral Regiment, 3rd Marine Division, alongside Philippine marines with 4th Marine Brigade, Philippine Marine Corps, successfully transported personnel, sustainment supplies and advanced coastal defense systems aboard Philippine vessels during bilateral littoral mobility training in Northern Luzon, June 21-22, 2026, in support of the Philippine-led exercise, KAMANDAG 10. The training enhanced the ability of U.S. and Philippine forces to move and sustain personnel and equipment across complex maritime terrain while strengthening interoperability and combined readiness.

title: Employment Before, During, and After Prison in California
summary: Broadening access to the labor market among released prisoners has long been a policy goal, but research in this area has been limited. This report looks at employment histories of Californians recently released from prison and finds that they have struggled to find work. Our findings also point to policies that could promote employment among this population.

source: Publication Archive - Public Policy Institute of California
title: Policy Brief: Employment Before, During, and After Prison in California
summary: An examination of key aspects of employment among Californians with prison histories indicates that people released from prison before the pandemic struggled to find work. It also points to policies that could promote employment among this population.

title: Annual Report on Tax Exemptions for Medicinal Cannabis
summary: Chapter 920 of 2024 (AB 2555, Quirk-Silva) renewed an existing tax exemption for certain donations of medical cannabis products. The law directs our office to submit an annual report containing data on three outcomes related to the exemptions: the number of medicinal cannabis patients served, the amount of medicinal cannabis products donated, and the amount of tax revenue lost. This report fulfills that statutory requirement for 2025.

source: Publication Archive - Public Policy Institute of California
title: Policy Brief: Lessons from California’s Early Literacy Policies
summary: Reading plays a critical role in educational outcomes and long-term success, but many students lack the skills they need to thrive. Under new literacy policies, reading skills in California have greatly improved, suggesting that growth is possible when the state invests in professional development and support for struggling students.

title: Defund Police is BACK: LA's Insane Attack on Cops Exposed (2026)
summary:
LA Council Bans Pretextual Stops — Why This Endangers Everyone

The radical left never quit — they're just rebranding their war on law enforcement.

Mark Garrett exposes the truth behind Karen Bass, council members, and the latest moves that put citizens at risk while protecting criminals and illegal aliens. Spencer Pratt also drops truth bombs on the failed "treatment first" approach.

Watch the full episode live on Your LEO Nation and see why these policies are failing America’s streets.
